text:
- **[NMP #2865](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2865)** — Phase 0 subissue: bring `nmp-nip60` into workspace/CI, capability-gate the false `pay_invoice` stubs, demote the legacy `kind:17375` relay-tag authority. [...]     "summary": "publish_nutzap_info still writes legacy_relay_hint into the kind:10019 relays field the diff's own docs declare non-authoritative",
    "failure_scenario": "Every doc comment touched by this PR (lib.rs, mint_announce.rs, wallet_event.rs, nip60_wallet.rs, and the legacy_relay_hint() accessor) newly and emphatically asserts the kind:17375 relay tags are legacy residue that 'must never' be used as relay-selection source of truth — the authoritative set is kind:10019/NIP-65. But publish_nutzap_info still populates the *published* kind:10019 NutZap-info `relays` straight from self.legacy_relay_hint. kind:10019 relays are the ONE place NIP-61 treats this data as authoritative: senders read the recipient's kind:10019 relays to know where to publish kind:9321 nutzaps. A wallet decoded via from_wallet_event from a kind:17375 that had no relay tags gets an empty legacy_relay_hint, so publish_nutzap_info emits a kind:10019 advertising an empty relay set, and inbound nutzaps become undeliverable — the user silently receives nothing. The behavior is unchanged from before the rename (pre-existing quirk, not a code regression), but the PR's new contract now directly contradicts this write path, so it's the highest-value thing to flag." [...]     "summary": "The renamed `legacy_relay_hint()` public accessor has zero callers anywhere in the workspace (nmp-wallet doesn't exist yet); the rename preserved a getter nothing reads and wrapped it in a 7-line doc comment. Simpler: drop the accessor (and the getter's essay) until nmp-wallet actually consumes it.",
    "failure_scenario": "A `pub` getter that no code calls is a permanent maintenance tax: every future reader must reason about a non-authoritative accessor that returns nothing anyone uses, and because it's public it can't be safely removed later without a churny 'is this API load-bearing?' audit. Verified via `grep -rn '\\.legacy_relay_hint()'` across all crates — no hits." [...]     "summary": "The handle field `legacy_relay_hint: Vec<String>` duplicates `WalletConfig.legacy_relay_hint`, which the handle already holds inside `config: Arc<Mutex<WalletConfig>>`. Both `create_new` (line 112) and `from_wallet_event` (line 130) copy the same vec into two places. Simpler: return it from `config.lock()` and delete the redundant field.",
    "failure_scenario": "Two copies of the same state kept in sync by hand can diverge: a future edit that mutates the wallet's relay hint through `config` won't touch the handle's private copy, so the accessor silently returns stale relays while `build_wallet_event` (which reads `config.legacy_relay_hint`) writes the fresh ones." [...]     "summary": "Re-adding nmp-nip60 to workspace.members now compiles a mandatory ureq TLS HTTP client + 409-line synchronous Cashu mint client (src/cashu/client.rs) into every `cargo build`/`cargo test --workspace`/CI run, even though the PR's own docs redefine the crate scope to 'NIP mechanics only — event codecs, types, pure shape validation' with all networking in the not-yet-existent nmp-wallet. Sibling HTTP-using crates (nmp-nip57, nmp-nip05) gate ureq behind an optional `native` feature (`native = [\"dep:ureq\"]`); nmp-nip60 makes it unconditional. Cheaper: gate ureq + cashu::client behind an optional feature, or move client.rs to nmp-wallet, so builds that do not exercise minting skip ureq + rustls/ring compilation.", [...]     "summary": "Rename is skin-deep on the WRITE path: freshly-built kind:17375 events still emit `relay` tags from `legacy_relay_hint`. The design doc's Relay Acquisition section sanctions only PARSING legacy tags ('parse it only as a non-authoritative compatibility hint'), i.e. read-compat for old wallets — not emitting new ones. `WalletConfig::generate(mints, legacy_relay_hint)` + `build_wallet_event` keep producing the exact residue activation was supposed to stop creating. Deeper fix: new kind:17375 should carry no relay tags (nmp-wallet owns relay acquisition); the constructor should not take a relay-hint param at all.",
    "failure_scenario": "Every wallet created after this PR still bakes relay tags into its kind:17375. When nmp-wallet lands and treats kind:10019/NIP-65 as authoritative, these self-emitted 17375 tags become live legacy data forever, so the 'legacy' hint is perpetuated by new writes rather than being a finite decode-only compat surface — defeating the purpose of the rename." [...]     "failure_scenario": "The PR renames the field to legacy_relay_hint and adds doc comments everywhere insisting it 'must never be treated as the relay-selection source of truth' — the authoritative set is the user's kind:10019 relay tags. But publish_nutzap_info still sets `relays: self.legacy_relay_hint.clone()` on the kind:10019 event it publishes. legacy_relay_hint is populated from the wallet's kind:17375 `relay` tags (from_wallet_event). So the legacy hint is copied verbatim INTO the authoritative kind:10019 surface. Two concrete failures: (a) if the old kind:17375 carried stale relay tags, they are re-minted as the authoritative kind:10019 relay set, so nutzap senders resolving the recipient's kind:10019 will publish kind:9321 nutzaps to relays the recipient no longer reads and the recipient never sees/redeems them; (b) in the going-forward common case where kind:17375 has no relay tags, legacy_relay_hint is empty, so the published kind:10019 has zero relay tags — degrading recipients to NIP-65 fallback and losing wallet-scoped relay hints entirely. The docstring acknowledges this as an intentional default, so it may be by-design, but it directly contradicts the PR's stated 'drop legacy 17375 relay-tag authority' goal and is the strongest correctness/semantics candidate in the diff." [...]     "summary": "build_wallet_event re-emits legacy_relay_hint as `relay` tags on every rebuilt kind:17375, perpetuating the legacy residue the PR aims to sunset",
    "failure_scenario": "decode_wallet_event reads kind:17375 `relay` tags into legacy_relay_hint; build_wallet_event writes config.legacy_relay_hint back out as `relay` tags. So any decode->rebuild->republish round-trip (e.g. adding a mint and re-signing the wallet event) re-stamps the legacy relay tags onto the new kind:17375. This keeps the 'legacy design residue' alive indefinitely rather than letting it age out, and means downstream tooling that still (wrongly) reads kind:17375 relay tags keeps getting fed them. Low confidence / likely intentional round-trip fidelity, but it works against the PR's stated intent to deprecate the 17375 relay-tag surface." [...]     "summary": "publish_nutzap_info() seeds the AUTHORITATIVE kind:10019 NutZap-info relay tags from `self.legacy_relay_hint` (the renamed field), directly contradicting the doctrine this same PR adds — that the kind:17375 `relay` tags are a legacy, non-authoritative hint and must NEVER drive relay selection. NutZapInfo.relays flows into the kind:10019 event's `relay` tags (nutzap.rs:49 builds them), which senders treat as the source of truth for where to publish NutZaps.",
    "failure_scenario": "A wallet decoded from a kind:17375 event carrying stale/legacy `relay` tags calls publish_nutzap_info(); the emitted kind:10019 advertises those stale relays as the recipient's authoritative NutZap-receipt relays. Senders zap to relays the recipient no longer reads, so incoming NutZaps are lost — the exact outcome the PR's new 'kind:10019 is authoritative, legacy_relay_hint is not' rule was written to prevent. The rename made the field name honest but left the one publish path that turns the hint into truth." [...] - Handle field: `nip60_wallet.rs:84  legacy_relay_hint: Vec<String>,`
- create_new: `nip60_wallet.rs:112  legacy_relay_hint: relays,` while config gets the same vec via `WalletConfig::generate(vec![mint_url...], relays.clone())` (line 101).
- from_wallet_event: `nip60_wallet.rs:130  let legacy_relay_hint = config.legacy_relay_hint.clone();` then `:136  legacy_relay_hint,`. Both places seeded from the identical source. [...] (c) Zero callers of the accessor — CONFIRMED. `grep "legacy_relay_hint()"` returns no call sites anywhere in the workspace; every `Self::legacy_relay_hint` hit (lib.rs:26, mint_announce.rs:15, nutzap_send.rs:75) is a doc-comment rustdoc link, not a call. The two real reads use the field directly, not the accessor. [...] VERDICT: CONFIRMED — crates/nmp-nip60/src/ownership.rs:97-108

The `owns` text on the exclusive `mechanism` claim `nip60.cashu_backend_adapter` — "Cashu proof/DLEQ/P2PK/rollover types **and the Cashu backend adapter seam surface consumed by nmp-wallet**" — is inconsistent with both the code state and the design doc, and all three sub-issues verify:

(1) DELETED code: `git diff master...pr2866` shows `crates/nmp-nip60/src/backend.rs` removed (82 deletions: the `WalletBackend` trait, `PayResult`, `WalletError`), plus `pub mod backend;` and `pub use backend::{PayResult, WalletBackend, WalletError};` removed from lib.rs. `ls backend.rs` = No such file. So the "seam surface consumed by nmp-wallet" phrase describes code that no longer exists in this crate after the PR.

(2) adapter-vs-seam conflation: design doc is explicit — line 101 "`WalletBackend` lives in `nmp-wallet`, not `nmp-core` and not `nmp-nip60`"; line 75 (Existing NIP Crates) nmp-nip60 owns "a Cashu backend adapter **for** the `nmp-wallet::WalletBackend` seam." The doc cleanly separates *adapter* (nip60's) from *seam* (nmp-wallet's). The claim's phrase "the Cashu backend adapter **seam surface** consumed by nmp-wallet" fuses the two and reads as claiming the seam — which the crate's own note[0] (ownership.rs:112-115) then contradicts: "the unified WalletBackend seam ... are owned by nmp-wallet ... not this crate." Internal tension between the `owns` text and its own note.

(3) bundling: one exclusive id bundles settled crypto-type ownership (Cashu proof/DLEQ/P2PK/rollover — legitimate, matches doc line 73) with the contested/nonexistent "seam surface" wording in a single `owns` string.

CAVEAT on severity / enforcement: this is a documentation-accuracy finding, not a functional or linker bug. Per crates/nmp-ownership/src/macros.rs:15 the exclusive linker symbol is `concat!("__nmp_own__", claim_type, "__", scope_kind, "__", scope_value, "__", context)` = `__nmp_own__mechanism__namespace__nmp-nip60__`. It keys ONLY on claim_type + scope kind/value/context — NOT on the `id` and NOT on the `owns` text (lib.rs:59-63 confirms collision is scope-symbol based). So `exclusive: true` here merely reserves the (mechanism, namespace=nmp-nip60) scope against another crate; the "seam surface" wording is purely descriptive prose and causes no collision. The finding is real (the prose misdescribes deleted code and conflates adapter/seam against the doc), but its impact is claim-text accuracy, not runtime/build behavior. [...] (1) DELETED code: `git diff master...pr2866` shows `crates/nmp-nip60/src/backend.rs` removed (82 deletions: the `WalletBackend` trait, `PayResult`, `WalletError`), plus `pub mod backend;` and `pub use backend::{PayResult, WalletBackend, WalletError};` removed from lib.rs. `ls backend.rs` = No such file. So the "seam surface consumed by nmp-wallet" phrase describes code that no longer exists in this crate after the PR.

(2) adapter-vs-seam conflation: design doc is explicit — line 101 "`WalletBackend` lives in `nmp-wallet`, not `nmp-core` and not `nmp-nip60`"; line 75 (Existing NIP Crates) nmp-nip60 owns "a Cashu backend adapter **for** the `nmp-wallet::WalletBackend` seam." The doc cleanly separates *adapter* (nip60's) from *seam* (nmp-wallet's). The claim's phrase "the Cashu backend adapter **seam surface** consumed by nmp-wallet" fuses the two and reads as claiming the seam — which the crate's own note[0] (ownership.rs:112-115) then contradicts: "the unified WalletBackend seam ... are owned by nmp-wallet ... not this crate." Internal tension between the `owns` text and its own note. [...] (b) Siblings DO gate ureq behind an optional `native` feature. CONFIRMED:
  nmp-nip57/Cargo.toml:
    `[features]` / `native = ["dep:ureq"]`
    `ureq = { version = "2", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["tls", "json"] }`
  nmp-nip05/Cargo.toml:
    `native = ["dep:ureq"]`
    `ureq = { version = "2", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["tls", "json"] }`
